name: Iron Monastery — The Library
description: The library is the mountain. Shelves cut into living rock, records wrapped in oiled cloth against the damp, organized by a system no outsider has decoded and possibly no insider has either — the monks navigate it by accumulated memory, which does not transfer. The oldest materials are in the deepest chamber: rolls of something not quite paper, script that predates both nations, written by people who watched the Cascade from the other side of it. At the reading table: a lamp, a chair worn to the shape of someone specific. Notes in small precise handwriting spread across one end, ink still dark. At the far end: fresh copies in careful transcription, a cipher key tacked to the wall. Someone has been translating the oldest texts into a language the living can read. The lamp is burned low in the way of a lamp that was not extinguished when work stopped for the night but when work simply stopped, which is a different kind of burn.
